requirements.text: WHEN the project browser panel opens, the system shall display a search input field with placeholder text 'Search projects...'
WHEN the user types in the project browser search input, the system shall filter the project list by case-insensitive substring match on project code OR project name within 50ms
WHEN the search query matches the current project code or name, the system shall exclude the current project from filtered results
WHEN no projects match the search query, the system shall display empty state message 'No projects match your search'
WHEN the user presses Escape while the project browser search input is focused, the system shall close the project browser panel
WHEN the project browser panel opens, the system shall focus the search input automatically
WHEN the user types a ticket key pattern matching {CODE}-{NUMBER} where CODE is 2-5 uppercase letters and NUMBER is 1-5 digits, the system shall recognize it as a cross-project ticket search
WHEN a cross-project ticket search is triggered, the system shall wait 300ms after the last keystroke before initiating the backend fetch
WHILE the cross-project fetch is in progress, the system shall display a loading state with spinner and up to 3 skeleton cards
WHEN the cross-project fetch completes successfully, the system shall display matching tickets with their project context (project code and name)
WHEN the cross-project fetch returns no results for a query (non-ticket-key pattern), the system shall display 'No tickets found matching {query}' with suggestion to check the ticket key
IF the cross-project fetch fails due to network error, the system shall display an error message with a retry option
WHEN a cross-project ticket key pattern (e.g., ABC-42) is searched and returns no results, the system shall display 'Ticket {CODE}-{NUMBER} not found'
WHEN the user types @{CODE} followed by space and search text in QuickSearch, the system shall recognize it as a project-scoped search
WHEN a project-scoped search is triggered, the system shall validate that the project code exists
IF the project code does not exist, the system shall display 'Project {CODE} not found' and not trigger a search
WHEN the project-scoped search is triggered with a valid project code, the system shall wait 300ms after the last keystroke before initiating the backend fetch
WHEN the project-scoped fetch completes successfully, the system shall display matching tickets from the specified project only
WHEN the user types text without special syntax in QuickSearch, the system shall filter current project tickets by key number, full code, or title (preserving MDT-136 behavior)
WHILE the user is in default current project mode, the system shall display the mode indicator 'In: {CODE}' where CODE is the current project code
WHILE a cross-project ticket key search is in progress, the system shall display the mode indicator 'Searching: {CODE}-{NUMBER}'
WHILE a project-scoped search is in progress, the system shall display the mode indicator 'In: {CODE}'
WHEN the user presses up or down arrow keys in QuickSearch, the system shall navigate through results across all sections
WHEN the user presses Tab in QuickSearch with multiple result sections, the system shall cycle focus between sections
WHEN the user presses Enter on a selected result, the system shall open the ticket or switch to the project
Cross-project ticket searches require backend fetch; tickets cannot be preloaded for all projects
Cross-project search debounce duration shall be 300ms maximum
Cross-project search results shall be limited to 5 per project, 15 total maximum
Loading skeleton shall render within 50ms of fetch start
No layout shift shall occur when results load
Must integrate with existing QuickSearch modal infrastructure (MDT-136)
Must integrate with existing ProjectBrowserPanel (MDT-129)
Must follow existing modal/overlay patterns per MODALS.md
Cross-project ticket cache shall use 5 minute TTL for recently fetched project tickets
Invalid project code in @syntax shows 'Project not found' message without triggering search
Non-existent ticket key pattern (e.g., ABC-999) shows 'Ticket ABC-999 not found' message
Empty search results show appropriate empty state per search mode
Network error during fetch shows error message with retry option
If current project is MDT and user types MDT-136, show the ticket in Current Project section (not excluded)
If user types @MDT while in project MDT, show all MDT tickets without exclusion (user explicitly requested current project)
WHEN a cross-project ticket is selected from search results, the system shall navigate to the ticket's own project URL using the ticket's project code, not the current project code
WHEN cross-project search results are displayed, the system shall exclude tickets from the current project to prevent duplicate display across current and cross-project sections

If a cross-project search API returns results for the current project, the client-side filteredCrossProjectResults memo must exclude them before rendering to avoid duplication with the current project section

scenarios.title: Project browser search filters projects and excludes current
scenarios.given: the project browser panel is open with multiple registered projects including the current project MDT
scenarios.when: the user types 'MDT' in the search input
scenarios.then: the project list filters by case-insensitive substring match and excludes the current project MDT from results

scenarios.title: Project browser search shows empty state when no matches
scenarios.given: the project browser panel is open with registered projects
scenarios.when: the user types a query that matches no project code or name
scenarios.then: the system displays 'No projects match your search' empty state

scenarios.title: Project browser Escape key closes panel
scenarios.given: the project browser panel is open
scenarios.when: the user presses Escape while the search input is focused

scenarios.title: Cross-project ticket key search loads and displays results
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open in project MDT
scenarios.when: the user types a valid ticket key pattern 'ABC-42'
scenarios.then: after 300ms debounce the system displays loading spinner with skeletons then shows matching tickets with project context

scenarios.title: Cross-project ticket key not found shows specific message
scenarios.when: the user types a ticket key pattern 'XYZ-999' that does not exist
scenarios.then: the system displays 'Ticket XYZ-999 not found' message

scenarios.title: Cross-project search network error shows retry option
scenarios.when: a cross-project search fails due to network error
scenarios.then: the system displays an error message with a retry option

scenarios.title: Project-scoped search with valid project code searches that project
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open in project MDT
scenarios.when: the user types '@ABC login' where ABC is a valid project code
scenarios.then: after 300ms debounce the system displays matching tickets from project ABC only

scenarios.title: Project-scoped search with invalid project code shows error
scenarios.when: the user types '@XYZ login' where XYZ is not a valid project code
scenarios.then: the system displays 'Project XYZ not found' and does not trigger a search

scenarios.title: Current project search preserves MDT-136 behavior
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open in project MDT
scenarios.when: the user types 'badge' without special syntax
scenarios.then: the system filters current project tickets by key number, full code, or title, and displays mode indicator 'In: MDT'

scenarios.title: Mode indicator reflects search context
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open in project MDT
scenarios.when: the user switches between search modes (default, ticket key, project-scoped)
scenarios.then: the mode indicator updates to show 'In: MDT', 'Searching: ABC-42', or 'In: ABC' accordingly

scenarios.title: Keyboard navigation with arrow keys moves through results
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open with multiple results across sections
scenarios.when: the user presses up and down arrow keys then Enter on a selected result
scenarios.then: the system navigates through results across all sections and opens the selected ticket

scenarios.title: Tab key cycles between result sections
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open with current project and cross-project results
scenarios.then: focus cycles between result sections (current project to cross-project)

scenarios.title: Cross-project ticket selection navigates to correct project URL
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open in project MDT and a cross-project search for ABC-42 returns results from project ABC
scenarios.when: the user selects ticket ABC-42 from the cross-project results
scenarios.then: the system navigates to the ABC project URL with ticket ABC-42 selected, not the MDT project URL

scenarios.title: Cross-project search results exclude current project tickets to prevent duplication
scenarios.given: the QuickSearch modal is open in project MDT and a cross-project search returns results including MDT tickets
scenarios.then: tickets from project MDT are excluded from the cross-project results section and only appear in the current project section

obligations.title: Expose a single backend search endpoint that handles ticket-key lookup and project-scoped search without all-project preloading
Define typed search request, response, mode, result-limit, and project-code validation contracts in the shared domain boundary
Route @CODE query syntax through project validation, 300ms debounce, backend fetch, and scoped result rendering
Keep cross-project search cache client-owned with 5 minute TTL and request dedupe, never as all-project preloading
Apply a maximum 300ms debounce before cross-project backend fetches for ticket-key and project-scoped modes
Add instant client-side ProjectBrowserPanel filtering with autofocus, Escape close, current-project exclusion, and empty state
Apply current-project exclusion only to ProjectBrowserPanel search, not explicit QuickSearch ticket-key or @current-project searches
Detect QuickSearch modes in one hook: default current-project text, exact ticket-key pattern, and @CODE-space scoped search
Integrate cross-project states into the existing QuickSearch modal while preserving MODALS.md overlay, close, and keyboard conventions
Render spinner and up to 3 skeleton cards within 50ms of cross-project fetch start without result layout shift
Display contextual empty, not-found, invalid-project, and retry states for each search mode
Display mode indicators for default, ticket-key, and project-scoped QuickSearch contexts
Preserve arrow, Tab section cycling, Enter selection, and close behavior across merged current-project and cross-project result sections
Cover project browser filtering and QuickSearch current-project, ticket-key, project-scoped, loading, empty, error, and keyboard flows in E2E tests
Unit-test query mode detection, current-project filtering preservation, debounce/cache behavior, and project-code validation branches
Keep QuickSearch and ProjectBrowserPanel specs and mockups aligned with @CODE syntax, backend search endpoint, modal patterns, and empty/loading states
Route cross-project ticket selection to the ticket's own project URL by threading targetProjectCode through onSelectTicket callback from QuickSearchModal to App.tsx handleTicketClick
Exclude current project tickets from cross-project search results via filteredCrossProjectResults useMemo in QuickSearchModal, preventing duplicates between current and cross-project sections

testPlans.title: useCrossProjectSearch debounce, cache, loading, dedupe, retry
useQuickSearch query mode detection and parsing
Cross-project ticket navigates to correct project URL and results are deduplicated

tasks.title: Create Missing Infrastructure Stubs + Verify Builds
QuickSearch Ticket Key + Current Project + Mode Indicators
Implementation bug fixes: cross-project navigation, deduplication, and E2E test corrections
